waypoints.name: Convict Lake Campground
waypoints.notes: Base camp both nights. 48 reservable sites via Recreation.gov. Flush toilets, potable water, bear lockers. Paid showers at Convict Lake Resort. $35/night.

waypoints.name: Brees Lookout
waypoints.notes: Interpretive overlook at Hot Creek Geological Site. Panoramic views of Hot Creek canyon, steaming geothermal pools, and Mammoth Mountain. 0.6 mile out-and-back trail descends into the canyon. Free, no fee.
warnings: FOREST ORDER: No swimming, no entering water — geothermal temps can exceed 200°F.

waypoints.name: Hot Creek Geologic Site
waypoints.notes: Interpretive trail, steaming geothermal pools, canyon views. Day use only, opens 6am. Free, no fee.
warnings: FOREST ORDER: No swimming, no entering water, no being within 10 feet of Hot Creek. Effective through Dec 31 2026. Citation risk.
No dogs in the water — geothermal temps unpredictable

waypoints.name: The Rock Tub Hot Springs
waypoints.notes: Single rock/cement tub, ~100°F, fits 6-8 people. No hiking required — drive right up. No fee, BLM land. Camping prohibited at tub itself. Brushes on-site to clean algae.
warnings: Whitmore Tubs Road is dirt — can be muddy/impassable post-storm. Check conditions morning of.
Keep dog out of water — small dogs are heat-sensitive, temps vary

waypoints.name: Lake Crowley Stone Columns
waypoints.notes: Columnar basalt formations on the north shore of Lake Crowley. Roadside/short walk access. Volcanic geology from Long Valley Caldera.

waypoints.name: Mosquito Flat Trailhead (Little Lakes Valley)
waypoints.notes: Little Lakes Valley trail: 7.3 mi RT, ~1,050 ft gain. Chains of alpine lakes — Mack, Marsh, Heart, Long, Chickenfoot. Loop option via Morgan Pass adds mileage. No water at trailhead — bring everything.
warnings: Parking fills by 8:30am on clear weekends — arrive before 8am or park lower and walk extra 0.25–0.5 mi
Snow on trail post-storm — microspikes required in May
Altitude: 10,230 ft — take it slow if coming from sea level
Upper Rock Creek Road open as of 4/21/26 — verify status if weather changes

waypoints.name: Big Pine Creek North Fork Trailhead
waypoints.notes: Big Pine Lakes trail: 9.6 mi RT to First + Second Lake, ~2,358 ft gain. Glacial blue lakes, Temple Crag views, Palisade Glacier visible from upper route. Overnight requires Inyo NF wilderness permit.
warnings: Snow on trail past mile 3–4 in May — microspikes required
First Lake may be frozen in early May
Glacier Lodge Campground likely not open until late May — do not rely on it
Day-use parking fee (~$6) — bring cash or card
AllTrails recommends June–October for best conditions

routes.label: Day 1 — Geology Crawl + Little Lakes Valley
